elements.title: Ritva Auvinen
elements.contents: ... that opera singer Ritva Auvinen (pictured) also worked as a gymnastics and swimming teacher?

elements.title: Australasia
elements.contents: ... that "Australasia" by William Charles Wentworth, the first book of verse by a native-born Australian, "celebrates the development of a new Britannia in another world"?

elements.title: Edward A. Brudno
elements.contents: ... that Vietnam War prisoner Edward A. Brudno was added to the Vietnam Veterans Memorial decades after his death following a Department of Defense ruling?

elements.title: Girls' Last Tour
elements.contents: ... that the post-apocalyptic manga Girls' Last Tour has been described as an iyashikei ('healing') story?

elements.title: Takanashi Kiara
elements.contents: ... that the VTuber Takanashi Kiara has attempted to take part in the Eurovision Song Contest multiple times?

elements.title: 1946 Representative Council election in Bangui
elements.contents: ... that Europeans and Africans voted in different buildings in the 1946 Representative Council election in Bangui?

elements.title: Blanca Quiñónez
elements.contents: ... that Blanca Quiñónez, the first player from South America in the history of UConn women's basketball, speaks the Molisan dialect alongside Spanish, English, and Italian?

elements.title: American Furniture Warehouse
elements.contents: ... that it's "none of your damned business" how much American Furniture Warehouse paid Billy Carter?
